assertion_results.text: The plan references checking out TC-9005 (the Target Branch) before creating the task branch — includes git checkout TC-9005 or equivalent, not git checkout main (constraint 3.1, Step 5)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 23-30 show: 'git checkout TC-9005' followed by 'git pull', then 'git checkout -b TC-9205' to create the task branch from the target branch. No reference to checking out main.

assertion_results.text: The plan specifies opening a PR with --base TC-9005 targeting the feature branch, not --base main (Step 10)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 36-38 show: 'gh pr create --base TC-9005 --head TC-9205 --title "feat(migration): add migration to drop advisory status column" --body "..."'. Line 39 explicitly states: 'The PR targets the TC-9005 feature branch (not main), since that is the Target Branch specified in the task.' Line 73 reiterates: 'The PR would target TC-9005 as the base branch via --base TC-9005.'

assertion_results.text: The plan names the task branch TC-9205 (the task issue ID), not TC-9005 (the feature branch) — the task branch is distinct from the Target Branch (constraint 3.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 30: 'git checkout -b TC-9205' creates the task branch. Line 33 clarifies: 'This creates a new branch TC-9205 based on the TC-9005 feature branch.' The task branch (TC-9205) is clearly distinct from the target branch (TC-9005).

assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 1 of: migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, migration/src/lib.rs (constraint 1.5, Steps 4-9 unaffected by Target Branch)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 87-95 under 'Pre-Implementation Verification' explicitly reference all three files: (1) 'Confirm that entity/src/advisory.rs does NOT reference a status column' (line 89), (2) 'Read migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs to understand the exact migration pattern' (line 91), (3) 'Read migration/src/lib.rs to understand how migrations are registered' (line 93). Additionally, conventions.md references analysis of these files in its headings for 'Migration Conventions (from sibling: migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs)' and 'Entity Conventions (from sibling: entity/src/advisory.rs)'.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 57: 'The commit would include the trailer: --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"'

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9205 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 44-55 show the commit message: 'feat(migration): drop deprecated status column from advisory table' which follows Conventional Commits format (type=feat, scope=migration, description). The footer on line 54 contains 'Implements TC-9205' referencing the task issue ID.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying TC-9005 as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 17-19 under 'Target Branch and Branch Operations > Target Branch': 'The **Target Branch** specified in the task is `TC-9005`. This is a feature branch (not `main`), indicating this task is part of a feature-branch workflow where multiple tasks are implemented on a shared feature branch before being merged to main.'
